metadata.title: Quiz: Communications Systems and Signals
metadata.description: A quiz on NRZI encoding and communications fundamentals.

question.text: In NRZI encoding where a transition represents a ‘1’: if there is a transition at a given symbol period, what bit does that represent?

answers.answer: It depends on the previous bit
It depends on the clock frequency

question.explanation: In NRZI (Non-Return to Zero Inverted), a transition in the signal level encodes a ‘1’, while no transition encodes a ‘0’.

question.text: What is the key advantage of NRZI over standard NRZ encoding for long bit sequences?

answers.answer: NRZI handles long runs of 0s better by using bit-stuffing
NRZI can encode transitions to help with clock recovery
NRZI always requires fewer signal levels

question.explanation: NRZI encodes data as transitions rather than absolute levels. Version (a) is used for long runs of 1s; version (b) for long runs of 0s (often via bit-stuffing). The transitions aid clock recovery.
